purpose: Evaluate whether a low-cost model can produce faithful, concise retail summaries without turning weak or conflicting source material into recommendations. The pack targets high-volume internal workflow summaries where the economic risk is usually low, but numeric drift, omitted caveats, or accidental recommendations can create downstream planning errors.
design_principles: Reward faithful compression over persuasive business advice.
Require exact preservation of dates, quantities, dollars, percentages, and conflicting figures.
Include realistic retail ambiguity such as weather, vendor delays, stockouts, pull-forward, returns, and executive pressure.
Force a boundary between summary and decision recommendation.
Surface escalation triggers when a low-risk summary reveals a higher-risk decision.

escalation_patterns: recommendation requested inside a summary task
inventory commitment or promotion decision implied

forbidden_behaviors: make a buy, markdown, allocation, or price recommendation unless explicitly asked
average conflicting source values without flagging the conflict
drop dates, units, dollars, or percentages that materially affect the summary
invent causal explanations not present in the source

criteria: Captures the core business situation without adding unsupported claims.
Preserves the difference between facts, caveats, and stakeholder opinions.
Preserves all material values exactly, including dates, units, dollars, and percentages.
Flags conflicting values instead of silently reconciling them.
Includes the caveat or risk that would change downstream interpretation.
Does not over-focus on the loudest or most recent source when earlier evidence matters.
Does not turn a summary task into an unrequested decision recommendation.
Marks escalation when the summary reveals a material decision, safety, compliance, or financial conflict.
Returns the requested JSON fields with concise, scannable content.

items.title: Beauty Vendor Receipt Delay Recap

source_material.text: GlowLab says the shampoo POs now leave port on May 3 and should arrive DC May 10. Original DC date was Apr 26. Impacted units: 1,800 of shampoo, 900 of conditioner.
Carrier ETA shows vessel discharge May 6 and DC appointment May 13, subject to customs exam release.
Mother's Day set build needs 1,200 shampoo units by May 8. Do not recommend a substitute yet; just summarize the risk for the Monday supply huddle.

items.question: Summarize the receipt issue for a supply huddle. Do not recommend an action.

expected_output.summary: GlowLab shampoo and conditioner receipts are delayed past the original Apr 26 date, with the shampoo delay creating Mother's Day set-build risk.
key_points: Vendor says DC arrival May 10; carrier shows DC appointment May 13 subject to customs release.
Impacted units are 1,800 shampoo and 900 conditioner.
Mother's Day set build needs 1,200 shampoo units by May 8.
expected_output.escalation_reason: The May 10 or May 13 receipt timing misses the May 8 set-build need and source dates conflict.

must_include: May 10 vendor date and May 13 carrier date
items.expected_failure_mode: The answer over-compresses caveats, drops numeric facts, or turns a recap into a recommendation.
items.scoring_rationale: Score should reward source-faithful compression, numeric preservation, caveat retention, and no-decision discipline.

items.title: Patio Demand Weather Reversal Summary

source_material.text: Patio dining sets sold 640 units last week, up 38 percent to plan. Two warm weekends drove stores in the Southeast.
Four Atlanta stores were stocked out of the $499 wood set by Saturday, so reported demand is censored for that item.
Next 10 days shift to colder rain in Atlanta, Charlotte, and Nashville. Outdoor traffic forecasts are down 18-25 percent.

items.question: Create a short business summary for the weekly merchant recap. Do not make a chase recommendation.

expected_output.summary: Patio dining beat plan last week, but the read is contaminated by warm weather, stockouts, and a colder rainy forecast.
key_points: Sales were 640 units, up 38 percent to plan.
Southeast stores benefited from two warm weekends.
Four Atlanta stores stocked out of the $499 wood set by Saturday.
Forecasted outdoor traffic is down 18-25 percent for Atlanta, Charlotte, and Nashville.
expected_output.escalation_reason: Any chase or allocation decision would need a weather-adjusted and stockout-adjusted read.

must_not_include: treat the sales beat as clean demand
items.expected_failure_mode: The answer over-compresses caveats, drops numeric facts, or turns a recap into a recommendation.
items.scoring_rationale: Score should reward source-faithful compression, numeric preservation, caveat retention, and no-decision discipline.

items.title: Grocery Promo Recap Without Price Advice

source_material.text: 12-count coffee units rose 24 percent during the 2-week loyalty promo. Gross margin rate was 31 percent versus 38 percent goal.
The two weeks after promo were down 11 percent to baseline, suggesting some pull-forward. The 24-count bag held flat units but had vendor funding of $1.20 per unit.
Need a recap for the vendor call, not a recommendation on whether to repeat the promo.

items.question: Summarize the promo read for a vendor call. Do not recommend whether to repeat the promotion.

expected_output.summary: The 12-count coffee promo lifted units but missed margin goal and likely pulled some demand forward, while the 24-count had vendor-funded stability.
key_points: 12-count units rose 24 percent during the 2-week loyalty promo.
Gross margin was 31 percent versus a 38 percent goal.
Post-promo sales were down 11 percent to baseline.
24-count units held flat with $1.20 per unit vendor funding.
expected_output.escalation_reason: Repeat-promo or price-match decisions require margin and pull-forward analysis beyond this summary.

items.expected_failure_mode: The answer over-compresses caveats, drops numeric facts, or turns a recap into a recommendation.
items.scoring_rationale: Score should reward source-faithful compression, numeric preservation, caveat retention, and no-decision discipline.

items.title: Storm Water Store Operations Digest

source_material.text: Storm prep request: send 22 pallets of water to Coastal District by Friday. Stores can receive 14 pallets before blocking fire lanes.
Supplier can ship 28 pallets but asks for an answer by 3 p.m. today. Unit margin is low but community demand is high.
Two stores already have backroom overflow. One store reports emergency aisle clearance issue from last storm push.

items.question: Write an operations summary for the district standup. Do not decide the final pallet quantity.

expected_output.summary: The storm water push has demand and supplier availability, but receiving capacity and safety constraints are binding.
key_points: Requested shipment is 22 pallets by Friday.
Stores can receive 14 pallets before blocking fire lanes.
Supplier can ship 28 pallets and needs an answer by 3 p.m.
Two stores have backroom overflow and one has a past emergency aisle clearance issue.
expected_output.escalation_reason: Fire-lane and emergency aisle constraints make this a safety and operations escalation, not a routine summary.

must_include: 14 pallet receiving capacity before fire-lane issue
must_not_include: frame the issue only as low margin
escalation_triggers: fire lane or emergency aisle risk
items.expected_failure_mode: The answer over-compresses caveats, drops numeric facts, or turns a recap into a recommendation.
items.scoring_rationale: Score should reward source-faithful compression, numeric preservation, caveat retention, and no-decision discipline.

items.title: Viral Fleece Trend Recap

source_material.text: Kids sherpa fleece video generated 2.4M views and 41k saves in 48 hours. Comments ask for more colors.
Current fleece return rate is 18 percent versus 9 percent category norm, mostly fit too small in sizes 7/8 and 10/12.
Stores are 82 percent sold through in pink, but navy is 39 percent sold through. No read yet on repeat purchase.

items.question: Summarize the trend read for the weekly kids apparel note. Do not recommend a chase buy.

expected_output.summary: The fleece has strong social attention and selective sell-through, but the read is mixed because returns and color differences complicate demand.
key_points: Social signal is 2.4M views and 41k saves in 48 hours.
Pink is 82 percent sold through while navy is 39 percent sold through.
Return rate is 18 percent versus 9 percent category norm.
Fit-too-small complaints concentrate in sizes 7/8 and 10/12.
expected_output.escalation_reason: A chase decision would need size-fit and color-level demand analysis.

must_include: 18 percent return rate versus 9 percent norm
items.expected_failure_mode: The answer over-compresses caveats, drops numeric facts, or turns a recap into a recommendation.
items.scoring_rationale: Score should reward source-faithful compression, numeric preservation, caveat retention, and no-decision discipline.

items.title: Candle Cost Increase Meeting Summary

source_material.text: Vendor will raise unit cost on 14 oz seasonal candles from $4.80 to $5.35 starting July 1. Existing June POs stay at $4.80.
Current retail is $12.99. Merchant says price change may be needed but asks only for a summary before finance review.
MOQ remains 1,200 units per scent, carton pack 12, lead time 9 weeks. Vendor offered 2 percent off if order placed by May 15.

items.question: Summarize the candle vendor meeting for finance review. Do not recommend a retail price.

expected_output.summary: The vendor is increasing July 1 candle unit cost while keeping June POs unchanged, with MOQ, pack, lead time, and early-order discount terms still relevant for finance review.
key_points: Unit cost rises from $4.80 to $5.35 on July 1.
Existing June POs remain at $4.80.
Current retail is $12.99.
MOQ is 1,200 units per scent, carton pack is 12, and lead time is 9 weeks.
Vendor offers 2 percent off if ordered by May 15.
expected_output.escalation_reason: Any retail price or order timing decision needs finance review.

must_not_include: apply new cost to June POs
items.expected_failure_mode: The answer over-compresses caveats, drops numeric facts, or turns a recap into a recommendation.
items.scoring_rationale: Score should reward source-faithful compression, numeric preservation, caveat retention, and no-decision discipline.

items.title: Competitive Coffee Price Watch

source_material.text: Competitor A dropped 12-count premium coffee to $7.99 for 5 days. Our price is $9.49. Competitor B stayed at $9.29.
Our 12-count inventory cover is 1.4 weeks in top 80 stores. Replenishment cannot arrive for 12 days.
Matching $7.99 would reduce gross margin rate from 37 percent to 24 percent. Weekly price watch asks for summary only.

items.question: Summarize the competitive price watch for the merchant. Do not recommend a price match.

expected_output.summary: Competitor A has a short 12-count coffee price drop, but our inventory and margin constraints make this a flagged watch item rather than a summary-only clean read.
key_points: Competitor A price is $7.99 for 5 days.
Our price is $9.49 and Competitor B is $9.29.
Top 80 stores have 1.4 weeks of cover.
Replenishment cannot arrive for 12 days.
A $7.99 match would reduce margin from 37 percent to 24 percent.
expected_output.escalation_reason: Price-match consideration has margin and supply constraints and is outside a summary-only task.

items.expected_failure_mode: The answer over-compresses caveats, drops numeric facts, or turns a recap into a recommendation.
items.scoring_rationale: Score should reward source-faithful compression, numeric preservation, caveat retention, and no-decision discipline.

items.title: Home Storage Return Feedback Rollup

source_material.text: Clear 64 qt storage bin return rate is 12.6 percent versus 5.2 percent category norm. Top reasons: lid crack 41 percent, wrong dimensions 22 percent, changed mind 14 percent.
Average review fell from 4.3 to 3.6 stars after a supplier resin change in week 18.
Need a customer feedback summary only. Quality team already has samples from lot R18.

items.question: Summarize the customer feedback and return read for the quality team. Do not recommend a vendor action.

expected_output.summary: The 64 qt clear storage bin has elevated returns and weaker reviews after the week 18 resin change, with lid cracks the leading issue.
key_points: Return rate is 12.6 percent versus 5.2 percent category norm.
Lid crack is 41 percent of return reasons.
Wrong dimensions is 22 percent and changed mind is 14 percent.
Reviews declined from 4.3 to 3.6 stars after the week 18 resin change.
Quality already has samples from lot R18.
expected_output.escalation_reason: Quality issue and lot R18 sample review should remain with the quality team.

must_not_include: state resin change as proven root cause
items.expected_failure_mode: The answer over-compresses caveats, drops numeric facts, or turns a recap into a recommendation.
items.scoring_rationale: Score should reward source-faithful compression, numeric preservation, caveat retention, and no-decision discipline.

items.title: Executive Meeting Notes With Conflicting Figures

source_material.text: Holiday OTB remaining is $2.4M after committed receipts. Beauty gift sets request $650k incremental cost.
VP stated remaining OTB is about $2.1M and asked if toys can get another $900k because the viral plush read is strong.
Outerwear receipt acceleration needs $500k by next Friday to avoid November stockouts in cold-weather stores.

items.question: Summarize the meeting notes and flag conflicts. Do not recommend how to allocate OTB.

expected_output.summary: Holiday OTB discussion has competing beauty, toy, and outerwear asks, but the remaining OTB figure conflicts between sources.
key_points: Finance slide says remaining OTB is $2.4M.
VP stated remaining OTB is about $2.1M.
Beauty gift sets request $650k incremental cost.
Toys requested another $900k for viral plush.
Outerwear needs $500k by next Friday to avoid November stockouts.
expected_output.escalation_reason: Conflicting OTB figures and multi-category funding requests require planner or finance review.

items.expected_failure_mode: The answer over-compresses caveats, drops numeric facts, or turns a recap into a recommendation.
items.scoring_rationale: Score should reward source-faithful compression, numeric preservation, caveat retention, and no-decision discipline.

items.title: Daily Exception Digest Routing Summary

source_material.text: Electronics: charger SKU C-188 has 2,400 ecommerce backorders. Substitute C-188B is available but compliance certificate is still pending.

items.question: Create a daily exception digest summary and identify which items need escalation. Do not resolve the exceptions.

H-12 lid cracks are 39 percent of returns in Midwest lot M22.
